title: Improvement-Targeting Workflow
goal: Make symptom-to-remediation routing explicit so memory-derived and review-derived signals routinely turn into upstream improvements and then shrink, stub, or disappear instead of lingering as long-lived workaround notes.
non_goals: Build new automation or issue sync.
Force every signal into active work immediately.

active_milestone.scope: add the explicit improvement-targeting workflow to the shared memory and review contract surfaces, thread post-remediation shape into the review template, and lock the wording with focused payload assertions.

immediate_next_action: None. Milestone completed and archived after the symptom-to-remediation workflow landed in the shared memory and review docs and the focused package lanes passed.
invariants: Durable truth and improvement signals remain distinct.
The workflow stays advisory outside the managed bootstrap surface.
Promotion into roadmap or active planning remains explicit rather than automatic.
completion_criteria: The shipped memory and review docs define the symptom-to-remediation workflow explicitly.
The review template requires the intended post-remediation note shape.
Memory and planning payload assertions cover the new workflow contract.
drift_log: 2026-04-08: Promoted from GitHub issue `#8` after the repo had improvement-signal concepts and promotion tools but still lacked an explicit end-to-end workflow for turning symptomatic notes into upstream improvement work.
2026-04-08: Completed after defining the improvement-targeting workflow in memory and review surfaces, adding post-remediation note-shape guidance, and validating the updated payload assertions.
